findings.title: V4 pools with nonzero hooks bypass hook governance when hookData is empty
findings.suggested_fix: Treat the V4 PoolKey and hook address as governed even when swap hookData is empty. Require an allowlisted key for any V4 payload with a nonzero hooks address, or move to a pool-key allowlist for all V4 payloads, and have the solver/deploy manifests plus validation script assert every generated V4 route's required key is enabled before launch.

residual_risks: Static read-only inspection only; I did not run Foundry, Bun, or fork tests because the request was read-only and test runs can write cache/build artifacts.

No hardcoded private keys, mnemonics, raw RPC URLs, explorer API keys, or bearer tokens were found in the added public config, docs, scripts, router-ts files, or checked-in broadcast artifacts inspected.

Generated solver routes are marked productionExecutable=false and materialization requires caller-supplied near-term deadlines and per-leg/final minimums; production safety still depends on downstream callers using that materialization path rather than submitting fork-evidence artifacts directly.
testing_gaps: No negative test covers a V4 payload with hooks != address(0), hookData == 0x, and no approved pool/hook key.
No deployment-validation test proves required V4 hook or pool keys are configured on the deployed router when generated artifacts include hook-address V4 routes.
No checked-in CI secret-scan gate was observed for newly added deployment artifacts and docs.
